history.content:
<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. There is a safe open cell to my right, and the goal is visible further in that direction. Turning right is the safest move to begin approaching the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing right. The cell directly in front of me is a wall, so I cannot move forward. Turning left would face the lava cell above me, which is unsafe. Turning right will face the open floor cell below me, allowing safe movement on the next step and keeping a path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing down, and the cell directly in front of me is outside the visible safe corridor, while both left and right sides may offer progress. The known open path lies to my left relative to my current facing, so I should turn right to reorient toward that corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. There is lava to my right, so I should avoid turning toward it. Turning left is the safer choice to look for an open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is blocked by a wall, so moving forward is not possible. The open corridor is to my left side in the visible map, so I should turn left to face that safe direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible to the left, but I am currently facing a wall, so moving forward is blocked. Turning right would face lava nearby, while turning left will reorient me toward the open corridor leading toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible ahead to the right. The cell directly in front of me contains lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The safe open path is to the right, so I should turn right to face it.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am back in the corridor view facing a wall directly ahead, so moving forward is impossible. The goal lies to the left side of the corridor, and turning left will align me with the safe open path toward it while avoiding the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The view matches the earlier position: the cell directly in front is lava, so I must not move forward. The only safe progress toward the visible goal is through the open space on my right, so I should turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing a wall directly ahead, so I cannot move forward. The corridor and goal are to my left, and turning left will orient me toward the safe path while keeping away from the lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. The open path toward the visible goal lies to the right, making a right turn the best next step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
There is a wall directly ahead, so moving forward is impossible. The safe corridor and the visible goal are to my left, while lava is nearby in the middle column. Turning left is the correct next step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is lava, so I must not move forward. The visible safe route toward the goal is to the right, so turning right is the best move.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
There is a wall directly ahead, so moving forward is blocked. The open path and the goal are to my left, while lava lies in the central corridor. Turning left is the safest step toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The safe open space is to the right, and the goal is also in that direction, so I should turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>
